issues.detail: Done can be authorized by any origin/main commit mentioning the identifier, including an empty, unrelated, revert-only, old, or direct-to-main commit. It does not require the matching PR, recorded pull, project/repository, or PR author demanded by REQ-026. Batch closure merely by listing identifiers compounds the hole.
issues.fix: Require an explicit post-pull GitHub PR merge record matching immutable issue UUID, project/repository, pull epoch, assignee/author, nonempty issue-scoped diff, exact merge OID, and required checks. Prohibit direct-to-main completion and identifier-only batch closure, or define an equally strong reviewed completion manifest.

issues.detail: During an MCP outage the plan queues assignment while allowing work to continue and says attribution remains unaffected. A queued pull is not an explicit self-assignment, concurrent sessions can both claim it, and REQ-028 explicitly floors task binding to unattributed on task-system auth failure.
issues.fix: Permit continued task work only for a previously confirmed pull. New work during outage must use exploration/unattributed until an online compare-and-set claim succeeds; replay must never retroactively manufacture In Progress authority.

issues.detail: A cited local result line is self-asserted evidence: the run may use a dirty tree, another SHA, a narrowed command, or tests changed/deleted in the same commit. The plan also does not identify the authoritative verify-set revision or required CI check names.
issues.fix: Pin the verify-set manifest revision at pull, run its exact commands in a clean detached checkout of the merge SHA, record command, exit code, environment, manifest hash, and durable full-log artifact, and require named protected CI checks once available.

issues.detail: A later revert leaves the original identifier-bearing commit in history, so Done remains valid even though the work is removed. Force-push or squash history rewrites can instead remove or change cited SHAs without a cursor-based scan reliably noticing.
issues.fix: Protect main against force-push, treat linked PR reverts as invalidating events that reopen/reverify the issue, and trigger a full audit of all Done evidence whenever the stored main OID is no longer an ancestor.

issues.detail: Done, closing comment, and attachment are three separate MCP writes, so the promise of no partial writes is impossible. A crash after Done leaves missing evidence artifacts, while R1 sees Done and will not repair them; unbind ordering is also undefined.
issues.fix: Use a completion operation ID and resumable state machine, make every artifact discoverably idempotent, write Done last, and have reconcile repair or roll back incomplete completion bundles before unbinding.

issues.detail: Rechecking current state does not make comment or attachment creation idempotent, and check-then-create races still duplicate them. Ordering across queued assign, blocked, Done, and correction operations can regress state. A worktree-local JSONL is unavailable to sessions on other worktrees or machines and concurrent appends can corrupt it.
issues.fix: Define per-operation UUIDs, expected Linear version/state, causal ordering, embedded deduplication markers, atomic append, a single replay lease, retry/dead-letter semantics, and a durable per-machine spool with explicit cross-machine discovery or synchronization.

issues.detail: Binding is per worktree, not per session. Two live sessions in one worktree overwrite the same file; two worktrees can pull the same issue under the same founder assignee without Linear exposing a conflict. The cache lacks immutable issue UUID, repository, worktree/session identity, and pull revision.
issues.fix: Either enforce one live session per worktree and one lease per issue or store session-scoped bindings backed by an exclusive lease. Include immutable issue/project UUIDs, canonical repo, session/worktree ID, pull event/revision, and atomic compare-before-close checks.

issues.detail: Neither .task-binding nor loop/state/linear-queue.jsonl is covered by the current .gitignore. Binding metadata and queued comments can therefore be committed, merged between worktrees, or published; branch switches can also replace operational state.
issues.fix: Keep operational state outside tracked worktree contents, preferably under the Git common directory or OS application data, add explicit ignore rules, use restrictive permissions, and minimize queued content.

issues.detail: The cursor location, unit, update transaction, locking, and failure behavior are undefined. Concurrent reconcilers can race; advancing before all Linear work succeeds loses findings, while advancing afterward duplicates them. A rewritten history can orphan the cursor.
issues.fix: Store a locked per-canonical-repository main OID plus last-success metadata outside branches, verify ancestry, scan with overlap, make findings idempotent, advance only after durable processing, and fall back to a full rescan when ancestry fails.

issues.detail: R2 blindly reverting an unauthorized Done to In Progress itself violates the rule that only /next may set In Progress and may restore the wrong prior state—Todo, Blocked, or Canceled. The undefined blocked state/label and its clearing path can create another status authority.
issues.fix: Record and restore the last authorized state rather than hard-coding In Progress. Model blockers as lifecycle-independent records or labels with an explicit transition table, and never synthesize In Progress without a valid pull event.

issues.detail: State snapshots cannot detect unauthorized transitions reliably. A manual Done survives if any old matching commit exists, and an unbound manual In Progress or other status move may never be inspected by cheap reconcile. No actor owns corrections, no audited override exists, and nightly execution contradicts the no-daemon design unless something actually schedules it.
issues.fix: Reconcile Linear activity events against an authorized-transition ledger, cover every status transition, define founder-owned correction and explicit audited override commands, and specify a real trigger such as first session of each day plus a last-success SLA and alert.

issues.detail: A project move only produces a warning. The stale binding continues stamping the old hierarchy, and E3 still permits Done because it compares only the issue. Team moves can change the human identifier; label, milestone, dependency, priority, cancellation, or verify-set changes are not covered by cheap reconciliation.
issues.fix: Bind immutable Linear UUIDs and a structural revision. Refuse completion after structural drift until an explicit rebind/migration updates the decomp manifest, aliases, hierarchy, and pull record; allow metered requests to continue without turning telemetry into a gate.

issues.detail: The plan depends on canonical loop/decomp files, issue-ID maps, verify sets, and an existing drift check, but loop/decomp does not exist. Milestone mapping, dependency representation, sync-stamp discipline, conflict direction, and ownership of each field are undefined, so R4 and E2 are not implementable.
issues.fix: Make decomposition materialization a prerequisite. Define a versioned manifest containing REQ/AT ownership, issue/project UUIDs and identifiers, milestone, labels, dependencies, verify commands, and sync stamp; state precisely which fields Linear versus the repo owns and implement atomic create/reconcile rules.

issues.detail: A UserPromptSubmit XML fragment is prompt content, not guaranteed structured metadata on every metered request. Tool continuations, subagents, Lovable calls, and other downstream requests may not receive it. With no append-only usage log, the plan cannot reconcile task attribution to request/token records as REQ-034 requires.
issues.fix: Treat the prompt stamp as advisory unless the client sends validated structured binding metadata consumed by the gateway for every request ID. Define inheritance for subagents/tools and separate Lovable/project categories, and record the metadata in the single append-only token usage log without inspecting prompt bodies.

issues.detail: Mutable Linear-derived values are injected into every model prompt without an escaping or validation rule. Quotes or markup can break the envelope or act as prompt injection, and the model can echo the stamp into generated commit or PR text while repeated stamps add context/token noise.
issues.fix: Use immutable allow-listed identifiers, strict enum/regex validation and escaping, a non-user metadata channel where possible, one binding context per request rather than accumulating tags, and scrub the tag from generated artifacts.

issues.detail: Removing the GitHub integration silently loses open-branch/PR backlinks, PR author and review context, merge-event identity, and rewrite/revert observation. A post-Done commit attachment restores only a final link, while git log alone cannot reliably recover PR author or checks after squash/rebase.
issues.fix: Add session-driven GitHub API inspection: record and attach the PR when opened, persist PR node/number, author, base/head and merge OID, verify exact check suites at /done, and have full reconcile poll both open and merged linked PRs plus reverts.

issues.detail: The plan says Linear entirely drives build work, but repository instructions mandate a TaskMaster task before every edit and mirrored in_progress/testing/done transitions. That creates a second task lifecycle and makes /next no longer the sole operational pull authority.
issues.fix: Before adoption, replace or scope out AGENTS.md TaskMaster requirements for this buildout, or explicitly define a one-way compatibility bridge and field authority so TaskMaster cannot independently assign or close work.

issues.detail: The design playbook mandates design-batch-N commit prefixes but does not require the Linear identifier. Following it literally makes Design-project commits invisible to E1 and R1.
issues.fix: Define a compatible composite commit format, for example design-batch-N: AI4-123 ..., and update every existing workflow that mandates a different prefix before Linear-driven execution begins.
